events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **fail**, high risk. An ABS-to-PVC transition can't be solvent-welded with ordinary ABS or PVC cement — the two plastics are chemically incompatible, so the joint needs either a manufacturer-listed ABS-PVC transition cement (ASTM D3138) or an approved mechanical/elastomeric coupling. Since the work is only rough-complete (not yet closed in), the fix is to cut out and remake the joint correctly before cover-up and re-inspection.
